headline: Foreign Worker Children Denied Schooling Despite 120,000 Eligible
context: Approximately 120,000 children of documented foreign workers have no access to public education in Malaysia.

cards.big: 120,000 children of documented foreign workers cannot enroll in public schools
cards.sub: Education Act 1996 restricts public school access to Malaysian citizens

cards.big: Alternative learning centers run by NGOs serve only 18,000 of 120,000 eligible children
cards.sub: Remaining 102,000 children receive no formal education whatsoever

cards.big: CRC Article 28 guarantees education rights for all children regardless of nationality
cards.sub: Malaysia ratified CRC in 1995 but maintains reservations on education access

cards.big: Thailand provides 12 years free education to all children including migrant workers' children
cards.sub: Inclusive policy reduces long-term social costs of uneducated populations

cards.big: Denying education to children who live among us creates a permanent underclass within our borders
Should children of documented workers be allowed to attend public schools
